rationale: Assessed B2 — dual-pillar activation. Geo pillar: Iran has taken confirmed legislative action to impose transit fees on Strait of Hormuz energy carriers, with corroborated reporting from The Guardian, NEPM, and CSIS confirming direct delays to EU offshore wind component shipments. The Mediterranean supply corridor and North African logistics route — both named AeroGrid dependencies — sit inside this disrupted maritime theatre. Palermo manufacturing (HIGH criticality) faces material supply chain exposure. Cyber pillar: CISA/FBI/NSA joint advisory (June 2025) confirms MuddyWater (MOIS) pre-positioned backdoors inside critical infrastructure supply chain firms, and CyberAv3ngers (IRGC) has deployed IOCONTROL malware against internet-exposed ICS/OT devices at scale. These actors operate in alignment with Iran's coercive posture. Intelligence gap noted: no confirmed wind-sector-specific IP exfiltration, but OT pre-positioning in analogous sectors is confirmed. Collection not thin (geo=4, cyber=3, threshold=3). Scenario match high-confidence System Intrusion (financial rank #3 globally). Combined exposure of HIGH-criticality manufacturing site on the disrupted corridor, active nation-state OT pre-positioning, and confirmed supply chain route leverage meets the ESCALATE threshold.
